perturbation.original_text: "Business" means the manufacture and sale of consumer products including cooking products, waste & storage products, and tableware by the Company and activities ancillary thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: "Business" means activities as determined solely by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Business' to 'activities as determined solely by the Company,' it introduces ambiguity and the removal of the specific definition creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2.3, which relies on the original definition to limit the scope of services. The original definition defines what constitutes a business and then it’s referred to set the scope. This new definition gives total freedom and creates a contradiction since it’s not clear what activities are part of the business. It creates a uncertainty in enforcement because of the omission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other than as expressly set forth on Exhibit A, Section 2.1, Exhibit B, or as agreed by the Parties in writing, in no event shall Provider be obligated to provide any Service to the Recipient for any purpose other than to facilitate, on a transitional basis, the Recipient's ability to conduct business as conducted immediately preceding the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Business' to 'activities as determined solely by the Company' introduces a significant ambiguity. The original definition provided a specific scope for 'Business,' which Section 2.3 relies upon to limit the Provider's obligations to services that facilitate the Recipient's ability to conduct that defined business. By removing the specific definition and granting the Company sole discretion, it contradicts Section 2.3 because the scope of required services becomes undefined and potentially unlimited, rendering the limitation in Section 2.3 unenforceable and contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall pay Provider (i) the fee for each Service set forth on Exhibit A or Exhibit B, (ii) Providers' and their Affiliates' reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in providing the Services, including the third-party fees and expenses that are charged to Recipient or their Affiliates in connection with provision of the Services (including any fees and expenses charged by subcontractors permitted to provide the Services under Section 2.2) but excluding payments made to employees of Provider or any of their Affiliates pursuant to Section 5.2, and (iii) any other fees as agreed to by the Parties in writing (collectively, the "Fees").
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall pay Provider (i) the fee for each Service set forth on Exhibit A or Exhibit B, and (ii) any other fees as agreed to by the Parties in writing (collectively, the "Fees").
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Providers' and their Affiliates' reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in providing the Services, including the third-party fees and expenses that are charged to Recipient or their Affiliates in connection with provision of the Services (including any fees and expenses charged by subcontractors permitted to provide the Services under Section 2.2) but excluding payments made to employees of Provider or any of their Affiliates pursuant to Section 5.2', it creates an in-text contradiction with Exhibit A and Exhibit B, which explicitly list various pass-through expenses. Removing that text, but not removing the refferals in Exhibits A and B creates uncertainty in enforcement, because that cost are reffered and described in the exhibits. Thus, this creates uncertainty in enforcement because of the omission.
perturbation.contradicted_text: G1.3 IT Service Category: General Pass-thru / Variable Costs Variable and Pass-thru costs Service fees for consumption or license maintenance as levied by vendors to RGHI based on RCP utilization of such services or licenses. All services in group 24 months from the Commencement Date Termination will occur as services are contracted directly with RCP All services costs are pass- through of actual third- party costs incurred in providing the service
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'Providers' and their Affiliates' reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses incurred in providing the Services, including the third-party fees and expenses that are charged to Recipient or their Affiliates in connection with provision of the Services (including any fees and expenses charged by subcontractors permitted to provide the Services under Section 2.2) but excluding payments made to employees of Provider or any of their Affiliates pursuant to Section 5.2' from the payment clause, while Exhibit A still lists "General Pass-thru / Variable Costs Variable and Pass-thru costs Service fees", creates a direct contradiction. The changed text no longer explicitly requires reimbursement for these costs, while the contradicted text (Exhibit A) still refers to them as pass-through costs, creating an inconsistency in whether these costs are reimbursable, leading to unenforceability issues due to the Omissions – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party covenants and agrees that, without any additional consideration, it shall execute and deliver, or shall cause its Affiliates to execute and deliver, such documents and other papers and shall take, or shall cause its Affiliates to take, such further actions as may be reasonably required to carry out the provisions of this Agreement and give effect to the transactions contemplated by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party covenants and agrees that, without any additional consideration, it shall execute and deliver such documents as may be reasonably required to carry out the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or shall cause its Affiliates to execute and deliver, such documents and other papers and shall take, or shall cause its Affiliates to take, such further actions...and give effect to the transactions contemplated by this Agreement', the obligation of each party to ensure its affiliates comply with the contract is weakened, creating an in-text contradiction in Section 10.10, which references the Affiliates to be included in the contract obligations. This creates an uncertainty in enforcement because the Affiliates are reffered in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Party may assign this Agreement, or any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement (whether by operation of Law or otherwise), without the prior written consent of the other Party; provided, that notwithstanding the foregoing, any Party may assign any or all of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party to: (a) its Affiliates, (b) a purchaser of: (i) one or more of its Affiliates that is a Provider or Recipient under this Agreement; (ii) all or substantially all of the business or assets of one or more of its Affiliates that is a Provider or Recipient under this Agreement; or (iii) all or substantially all of such Party's business or assets, or (c) its financing sources solely for collateral purposes, in each case so long as the assignee agrees to be bound by the terms of this Agreement. Any permitted assignment shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the Parties and their respective heirs, successors and permitted assigns. Any attempted assignment of this Agreement, or the rights or obligations herein, not in accordance with the terms of this Section 10.10 shall be void. If an RGHI Affiliate Provider is no longer affiliated with RGHI due to the sale of all or substantially all of the business or assets of such Affiliate to a third party, RGHI shall cause such Affiliate to agree to continue providing the Services that it is providing at the time of such transaction consistent with the terms of this Agreement for the remaining Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text included an obligation for each party to ensure its affiliates comply with the contract. The changed text removes this obligation. The contradicted text references the Affiliates in the context of assigning the agreement. By removing the obligation of the party to ensure its affiliates comply with the contract, the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, as the contradicted text assumes that the Affiliates are tied to the contract obligations through the Party, creating an uncertainty in enforcement.
